titleStmt.title: Letter from Arthur Eagleston to the Under Secretary for Ireland, 8 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Arthur John Eagleston

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Arthur Eagleston (1870-1944) to the under secretary for Ireland regarding the appointment of Charles C. Broy as the Vice Consul for the United States in Dublin.Arthur John Eagleston was a civil servant who served as assistant secretary of the British Home Office from 1913-1932.At the time this letter was written Sir Robert Chalmers (1858-1938) was serving as the under secretary for Ireland. Chalmers was sent to Ireland as the replacement for Sir Matthew Nathan, who had resigned along with Augustine Birrell, his chief secretary, following the outbreak of the Easter Rising in 1916.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916-9773

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
HOME OFFICE, WHTEHALL. 8th June, 1916. Sir, The United States Ambassador
at this Court having notified to His Majesty's Government the appointment
of Mr. Charles C. Broy, of Virginia,
as Vice Consul for the United States
at Dublin, I am directed by the Secretary
of State to request that you will give directions to the proper authorities, at
your earliest convenience, to recognise that Gentleman in the capacity to
which he has been appointed, if there should be no objection to that course,
and that you will inform the Secretary of State whether such directions have
been given. I am,
Sir,
Your obedient Servant, A.J.Eagleston The Under Secretary, Dublin Castle.
